2605.17862.title: $\boldsymbol{f}$-OPD: Stabilizing Long-Horizon On-Policy Distillation with Freshness-Aware Control
2605.17862.paper_summary: Scaling on-policy distillation (OPD) for large language models (LLMs) confronts a fundamental tension: asynchronous execution is necessary for system efficiency, but structurally deviates from the ideal on-policy objective. To address this challenge, we theoretically decompose the objective discrepancy into rollout drift and supervision drift, capturing staleness in student rollout and teacher context, respectively. Building on this, we introduce a sample-level freshness score that quantifies the reliability of a buffered sample with respect to the on-policy objective. Guided by this signal, we further propose f-OPD, a novel framework that adaptively regulates stale-sample influence and constrains policy drift accumulated under asynchronous training. Across reasoning, tool-use, and coding-agent tasks of increasing interaction horizon, f-OPD consistently achieves task performance comparable to synchronous optimization while largely retaining the throughput advantages of asynchronous execution. Our results establish the first recipe for achieving a performance-efficiency trade-off in OPD, paving the way for long-horizon agentic post-training at scale.
2605.17862.paper_summary_zh: 擴展大型語言模型（LLMs）的在線政策蒸餾（OPD）面臨一個根本性的緊張關係：異步執行對系統效率是必要的，但在結構上偏離了理想的在線政策目標。為了解決這一挑戰，我們理論上將目標差異分解為回合漂移和監督漂移，分別捕捉學生回合和教師上下文中的陳舊性。在此基礎上，我們引入了一個樣本級的新鮮度分數，量化緩衝樣本相對於在線政策目標的可靠性。在這一信號的指導下，我們進一步提出了 f-OPD，一個新穎的框架，能夠自適應地調節陳舊樣本的影響，並限制在異步訓練下累積的政策漂移。在推理、工具使用和編碼代理任務中，隨著互動範圍的增加，f-OPD 始終實現了與同步優化相當的任務性能，同時在很大程度上保留了異步執行的吞吐量優勢。我們的結果建立了在 OPD 中實現性能與效率權衡的第一個方案，為大規模長期代理後訓練鋪平了道路。
2605.17862.author: Xianwei Chen et.al.
2605.17862.authors: Xianwei Chen, Shimin Zhang, Jibin Wu
